content:
# Acts Chapter 5 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF pages 230-231 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ACT_005_page_001.jpg, ACT_005_page_002.jpg

---

**1** Now a certain man, Ananias by-name, with Sapphira the wife of-him, sold a possession,

**2** and kept-back from the price, the wife also having-been-aware-of-[it], and having-brought a certain part, at the feet of-the apostles he-placed [it].

**3** But Peter said, "Ananias, on-account-of what did Satan fill the heart of-you, to-lie to the Holy Spirit and to-keep-back from the price of-the land?

**4** [While] remaining, did-it not remain to-you? And having-been-sold, in the your authority it-was? Why [is it] that you-placed in the heart of-you this deed? You-did not lie to-men but to-God."

**5** And hearing these words, Ananias, having-fallen-down, expired. And great fear came-upon all the [ones] hearing.

**6** And having-arisen, the young-men wrapped-up him and having-carried-out, they-buried [him].

**7** Now it-came-to-pass about an interval of-hours three, and the wife of-him, not knowing the [thing] having-happened, entered.

**8** And Peter answered to her, "Tell me if for-so-much you-gave-away the land?" And she said, "Yes, for-so-much."

**9** And Peter [said] to her, "Why [is it] that it-was-agreed-upon by-you to-test the Spirit of-[the] Lord? Behold, the feet of-the [ones] having-buried the husband of-you [are] at the door, and they-will-carry-out you."

**10** And she-fell immediately at the feet of-him and expired. And having-entered, the young-men found her dead, and having-carried-out, they-buried [her] with the husband of-her.

**11** And great fear came-upon the whole assembly and upon all the [ones] hearing these-things.

**12** Now through the hands of-the apostles were-coming-to-pass signs and wonders many among the people. And they-were all with-one-accord in the Porch of-Solomon.

**13** And of-the rest, no-one was-daring to-join-himself to-them, but the people were-magnifying them.

**14** And more [ones] believing were-being-added to-the Lord, multitudes both of-men and of-women,

**15** so-as even into the streets to-carry-out the sick and to-place [them] upon small-beds and mats, in-order-that, of-Peter coming, even the shadow might-overshadow some-one of-them.

**16** And also the multitude of-the surrounding cities was-coming-together into Jerusalem, bringing sick [ones] and [ones] being-troubled by unclean spirits, who were-being-healed all.

**17** And having-arisen, the high-priest and all the [ones] with him -- the existing sect of-the Sadducees -- were-filled [with] jealousy,

**18** and they-laid the hands upon the apostles and placed them in public custody.

**19** But an angel of-[the] Lord during the night having-opened the doors of-the prison, and having-led them out, said,

**20** "Go and having-stood, speak in the temple to-the people all the words of-this life."

**21** And having-heard, they-entered at-dawn into the temple and were-teaching. Now having-arrived, the high-priest and the [ones] with him, they-called-together the council and all the council-of-elders of-the sons of-Israel, and they-sent to the prison [for] them to-be-brought.

**22** But the officers having-arrived did not find them in the prison, and having-returned, they-reported,

**23** saying, "The prison we-found having-been-shut in all security, and the guards standing before the doors; but having-opened, inside no-one we-found."

**24** Now as they-heard these words, both the captain of-the temple and the chief-priests were-perplexed concerning them, what this might-become.

**25** And someone having-arrived reported to-them, "Behold, the men whom you-placed in the prison are in the temple, standing and teaching the people."

**26** Then having-gone-away, the captain with the officers brought them, not with violence, for they-were-fearing the people, lest they-might-be-stoned.

**27** And having-brought them, they-stood [them] in the council. And the high-priest questioned them,

**28** saying, "With-a-command we-commanded you not to-teach upon this name, and behold, you-have-filled Jerusalem [with] the teaching of-you, and you-intend to-bring upon us the blood of-this man."

**29** And having-answered, Peter and the apostles said, "It-is-necessary to-obey God rather than men.

**30** The God of-the fathers of-us raised Jesus, whom you laid-hands-on, having-hung [him] upon a tree.

**31** This-[one], God exalted [to] the right-[hand] of-him [as] Leader and Savior, to-give repentance to-Israel and forgiveness of-sins.

**32** And we are witnesses of-these words, and the Holy Spirit, which God gave to-the [ones] obeying him."

**33** And the [ones] having-heard were-cut-to-the-heart and were-intending to-kill them.

**34** But having-stood-up, a certain [one] in the council, a Pharisee, Gamaliel by-name, a teacher-of-the-law honored by all the people, commanded to-make the men outside for-a-little [while],

**35** and he-said to them, "Men, Israelites, take-heed to-yourselves upon these men, what you-are-about to-do.

**36** For before these days Theudas rose-up, saying himself to-be someone, to-whom a number of-men, about four-hundred, was-joined. Who was-killed, and all as-many-as were-being-persuaded by-him were-dispersed and came to nothing.

**37** After this-[one], Judas the Galilean rose-up in the days of-the registration and drew-away people after him. That-one also perished, and all as-many-as were-being-persuaded by-him were-scattered.

**38** And the [things] now, I-say to-you, stand-away from these men and let them [be], because if this counsel or this work is of men, it-will-be-overthrown;

**39** but if it-is of God, you-are-not-able to-overthrow them -- lest perhaps also [as] God-fighters you-might-be-found." And they-were-persuaded by-him.

**40** And having-called-for the apostles, having-beaten [them], they-commanded [them] not to-speak upon the name of-Jesus, and they-released them.

**41** Indeed therefore they were-going from [the] face of-the council, rejoicing that on-behalf-of the name they-were-counted-worthy to-be-dishonored.

**42** And every day, in the temple and from-house-to-house, they did not cease teaching and proclaiming-good-news [of] the Christ, Jesus.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Verse 2:** The verb "enosphisato" (kept-back) is the same word used of Achan in Joshua 7:1 (LXX). Sinaiticus preserves this term clearly. The middle voice implies personal benefit from the deception.

2. **Verse 3:** Sinaiticus reads "eplerosen" (filled) in the aorist -- Satan's filling of Ananias's heart is presented as a completed act, contrasted with the Spirit's filling of believers elsewhere.

3. **Verse 4:** The rhetorical structure preserves two conditional clauses: "while remaining, did it not remain yours?" and "having been sold, was it not in your authority?" The double question form is preserved literally from the Greek.

4. **Verse 6:** Sinaiticus reads "neōteroi" (young men / younger ones) here, distinct from "neaniskoi" (young men) in verse 10. Both terms refer to younger members of the assembly who performed practical duties.

5. **Verse 9:** "Synephōnēthē" (it-was-agreed-upon) -- the aorist passive: "why was it agreed upon by you?" The verb root gives us "symphony" -- literally a sounding-together, hence an agreement or conspiracy between husband and wife.

6. **Verse 11:** This is the first occurrence of "ekklesia" (assembly) in Acts. Sinaiticus reads the word clearly here. I render it "assembly" rather than "church" to preserve the literal Greek sense of a called-out gathering.

7. **Verse 12:** Sinaiticus reads "en tē stoa Solomōntos" (in the Porch of Solomon). The location is consistent with Acts 3:11.

8. **Verse 15:** Sinaiticus reads "klinariōn kai krabattōn" (small-beds and mats). The diminutive "klinariōn" is preserved; "krabattos" is a Latin loanword (grabatus) for a poor man's pallet.

9. **Verse 17:** "Zēlou" (jealousy/zeal) -- Sinaiticus reads this clearly. The word carries both positive (zeal) and negative (jealousy/envy) connotations. In this hostile context, "jealousy" is the appropriate rendering.

10. **Verse 19:** Sinaiticus reads "angelos kyriou" (angel of [the] Lord) without the article before "kyriou" -- the anarthrous construction is preserved. The phrase echoes the LXX "angel of the Lord" theophanies.

11. **Verse 21:** Sinaiticus reads "tēn gerousian" (the council-of-elders) as distinct from "to synedrion" (the council/Sanhedrin). This appears to be either an explanatory apposition or a reference to an additional body of elders. The distinction is preserved.

12. **Verse 28:** "Parangelia parēngeilamen" -- the cognate dative construction "with-a-command we-commanded" is a Semitic intensifying idiom (cf. the same pattern in 4:17). Preserved literally.

13. **Verse 30:** "Kremasantes epi xylou" (having-hung upon a tree) -- this echoes Deuteronomy 21:22-23 (LXX). The word "xylon" means wood/tree, not "cross" (stauros). Sinaiticus preserves this Old Testament allusion clearly.

14. **Verse 31:** Sinaiticus reads "archēgon kai sōtēra" (Leader and Savior). "Archēgos" means leader, originator, or pioneer -- not merely "prince." It denotes one who leads the way, the same word used in Acts 3:15.

15. **Verse 33:** "Dieprionto" (were-cut-to-the-heart) -- literally "were being sawn through." This is a different verb than the one in Acts 2:37 ("katenygesan" -- were pierced). Here the reaction is rage, not repentance.

16. **Verse 34:** Sinaiticus identifies Gamaliel as "nomodidaskalos" (teacher-of-the-law). This is the elder Gamaliel (Gamaliel I), grandson of Hillel, one of the most respected Pharisaic teachers.

17. **Verse 36:** Sinaiticus reads "proselythē" (was-joined) -- the number "about four hundred" aligns with the Sinaiticus text. The historical Theudas reference has been much debated regarding chronology.

18. **Verse 39:** Sinaiticus reads "theomachoi" (God-fighters) -- a compound word preserved with a hyphen. This term appears only here in the New Testament. The warning carries enormous rhetorical weight in Gamaliel's speech.

19. **Verse 41:** Sinaiticus reads "tou onomatos" (the name) -- the absolute use of "the Name" without specifying "of Jesus" is notable, suggesting that "the Name" had already become a recognized designation among believers.

20. **Verse 42:** Sinaiticus reads "ton Christon Iēsoun" (the Christ, Jesus) -- the article with "Christ" preserves its titular force: "the Anointed One, Jesus." This is not yet a proper name but a messianic title applied to Jesus.

-- EMET
